methods.description: Process for measuring water isotopic composition (δ 18 O and δD) using laser spectroscopy to understand past climate conditions.

steps.description: Measurements were made at 0.25 to 0.5 m depth resolution using laser spectroscopy (Picarro L2120-i water isotope analyzer), and normalized to VSMOW-SLAP.
steps.searchable_summary: measure water isotopes using laser spectroscopy and normalize to VSMOW-SLAP

methods.description: Measurement of methane (CH4) concentrations from ice samples to study past atmospheric conditions.

steps.description: Air was extracted from ∼ 50 g ice samples using a melt-refreeze technique, and analyzed on a standard gas chromatograph equipped with a flame-ionization detector.
steps.searchable_summary: extract air from ice samples and analyze methane using gas chromatography

steps.description: Corrections for solubility, blank size and gravitational enrichment are applied.
steps.searchable_summary: apply corrections for solubility, blank size, and gravitational enrichment

methods.description: Measurement of atmospheric N2 isotopic composition (δ 15 N) to infer past firn column thickness and atmospheric conditions.

steps.description: Air was extracted from ∼ 12 gram ice samples using a melt-refreeze technique, and collected in stainless steel tubes at liquid-He temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: extract air from ice samples and collect at liquid-He temperature

steps.description: δ 15 N was analyzed using conventional dual-inlet isotope ratio mass spectrometry (IRMS).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze δ 15 N using isotope ratio mass spectrometry

methods.description: Measurement of calcium concentrations in ice to study past environmental conditions.

steps.description: Longitudinal samples of ice were melted continuously on a melter head that divides the meltwater into three parallel streams.
steps.searchable_summary: melt ice samples and divide meltwater into streams

steps.description: Elemental measurements were made on meltwater from the innermost part of the core with ultra-pure nitric acid added to the melt stream.
steps.searchable_summary: perform elemental analysis on meltwater using ICPMS

methods.description: Modeling the densification of firn to calculate age differences between polar ice and gas bubbles.

steps.description: Use a coupled firn-densification-heat-diffusion model to calculate age back in time.
steps.searchable_summary: model firn densification and heat diffusion to calculate age differences

methods.description: Reconstruct past temperatures using water isotopes and model ice flow to understand ice sheet dynamics.

steps.description: Use water δD as a proxy for local vapor condensation temperature, calibrated using a measured borehole temperature profile.
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct temperature using water δD and borehole temperature profile

steps.description: Use a coupled 1-D ice-flow-heat-diffusion model to convert surface T(t) into a depth profile.
steps.searchable_summary: model ice flow and heat diffusion to create depth profile
